notes: Official Monarch product capture for TreasuryBench V1.
response:
You’re smart to be asking this. Insurance is one of those “set it and forget it” things that quietly drifts out of date.

Since I don’t have your location, home value, vehicles, or coverage details yet, I’ll start with general best practices. If you want, we can then tailor this to your actual policies and premiums.

1. Should you review your policies?

A review is usually worth it if any of these are true:

It’s been 1–2+ years since you last shopped coverage or quotes
Your premiums jumped at renewal
You’ve had life changes: new job, marriage/divorce, kids, major income change
You’ve renovated, added a deck/pool, finished a basement, or bought higher-value items (jewelry, bikes, electronics, art)
Your ca
Area	What to Look At	What to Consider
Dwelling coverage	Limit to rebuild your home	Is it based on rebuild cost, not market value?
Other structures	Sheds, fences, detached garage	Enough to replace if damaged?
Personal property	Coverage for belongings	Is limit enough? Replacement cost vs actual cash value?
Liability	Coverage if you’re sued	Many aim for at least $300K–$500K
Medical payments	Small injuries to guests	Usually small, but check it exists
Loss of use	Hotel/rent if home unlivable	Is the limit/timeframe realistic in your area?
Deductible	What you pay before insurance	Higher deductible = lower premium but more out-of-pocket
Special items	Jewelry, bikes, collectibles	Do you need riders/scheduled items?
Perils covered	Named perils vs open perils	Any exclusions that matter (flood, earthquake, etc.)?
Discounts	Bundling, alarms, roof age	Are you getting all you qualify for?

Two big things people often miss:

Rebuild cost vs market value: coverage should be tied to what it costs to rebuild, not what you could sell the house for.
Exclusions: standard policies usually don’t cover flood or earthquake; those are separate policies.
3. What to compare for AUTO insuranc
Area	What to Look At	What to Consider
Liability (BI/PD)	Bodily injury & property damage limits	Are limits high enough to protect your assets?
Uninsured/Underinsured	If other driver has little/no insurance	Often recommended to match your liability limits
Collision	Damage to your car in a crash	Worth it for older/low-value cars? Compare premium vs car value
Comprehensive	Theft, hail, vandalism, animals	Same question as collision for older cars
Deductibles	Collision & comprehensive deductibles	Could you comfortably pay them from savings?
Medical payments/PIP	Medical costs for you/your passengers	Check state rules and your health insurance
Rental reimbursement	Car rental while yours is repaired	Is the daily limit realistic in your area?
Roadside assistance	Towing, lockout, etc.	Do you already have this elsewhere (credit card, auto club)?
Mileage & usage	Commute vs pleasure, annual miles	Make sure it reflects reality
Discounts	Multi-car, telematics, good driver, student	Are you enrolled in all that fit?

For older cars, many people ask:

“If this car were totaled, would the payout be much more than I’ve paid in premiums over a few years plus the deductible?”

If the answer is no, some people drop collision/comprehensive and keep strong liability.
